title: Smash Burger
yield:
2 servings (2 double burgers)
Makes 12 patties at approximately 45 g each (8 reserved for future use)
intro: The American smash burger is about crust and timing. It traces its roots to early 20th-century diners, especially in the Midwest, where cooks used hot steel griddles to produce thin, fast-cooking patties. Pressing the meat onto the surface creates a deeply browned crust while keeping the interior tender. This approach stays close to that original idea, with a focus on control, consistency, and a well-developed crust.

ingredientGroups.title: Patty Mix
items: 16 ounces Impossible ground “beef”
1 large egg white
½ cup panko breadcrumbs
1 tablespoon olive oil
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon onion powder
1 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
½ teaspoon table salt
½ teaspoon Sazonador Total seasoning

ingredientGroups.title: Onions
items: 1 medium yellow onion, halved and thinly slivered
1 teaspoon avocado oil
¼ teaspoon salt

ingredientGroups.title: Cooking
items: 1 tablespoon avocado oil

ingredientGroups.title: Assembly
items: 2 potato buns
Dill pickle slices
4 thin slices reduced-fat white American cheese
Light mayo or burger sauce

stepGroups.title: Build and Chill the Patty Mix
items: Keep the Impossible “beef” very cold throughout this step.
Place all patty mix ingredients in a chilled bowl.
Fold gently until evenly distributed. Do not knead or compress.
Press into a flat slab about 1 to 1½ inches thick.
Cover and place in the freezer for 12 to 15 minutes until firm but not frozen.

stepGroups.title: Portion and Form
items: Divide into 12 portions at approximately 45 g each.
Using a 3¾-inch ring mold, press into patties about 3/8 inch thick.
Apply just enough pressure to reach the edges without compacting tightly.
Place on a parchment-lined tray and refrigerate 20 to 30 minutes.
Transfer 8 patties to the freezer, separating layers with parchment.

stepGroups.title: Pre-Season the Onions
items: Place sliced onions in a bowl.
Add avocado oil and salt.
Toss thoroughly to coat.
Let rest 10 to 15 minutes at room temperature.
Separate layers with your fingers so they cook evenly.

stepGroups.title: Preheat the Oven and Warm the Buns
items: Preheat the oven to 275 degrees Fahrenheit.
Wrap the potato buns tightly in foil and place in the oven for 10 minutes to warm and lightly steam.
Do not open the foil until final assembly.

stepGroups.title: Preheat the Pans
items: Place one cast iron skillet over medium-high heat for the onions.
Place a second cast iron skillet over medium heat to begin warming.

stepGroups.title: Cook the Onions (Pan One)
items: Add the pre-seasoned onions to the medium-high pan and spread into a thin, even layer.
Cook 4 to 6 minutes, leaving undisturbed for 60 to 90 seconds at a time before flipping sections.
When the onions begin to soften and release moisture, increase the second pan to high heat.
When fond forms and onions are about halfway softened, deglaze the pan with a light sprinkle of water.
Scrape the pan to release browned bits and coat the onions.
Allow any moisture to fully evaporate.
Continue cooking to target texture: 60% soft and translucent, 30% lightly golden at edges, 10% still slightly firm.
Divide into four portions and reduce heat to low to hold.

stepGroups.title: Smash and Finish (Pan Two)
items: Add avocado oil to the hot pan and wipe into a thin film.
Place 4 cold patties onto the surface. Wait 8 to 10 seconds.
Press firmly for 5 seconds to maximize contact. Do not twist.
The patties may tear slightly during smashing or flipping. This is normal and will come back together when topped with cheese.
Cook 55 to 65 seconds until deeply browned with lacy edges.
Scrape confidently and flip.
Cook 20 to 30 seconds on the second side.
Transfer each patty directly onto an onion portion.
Press gently for 2 to 3 seconds to seat.
Top each patty with a slice of white American cheese and cover the onion pan for 20 to 30 seconds until melted.

stepGroups.title: Assembly
items: Remove buns from the oven and keep wrapped until the moment of use. Open the foil just before assembling. Place pickle slices on the bottom bun in a single layer. Add two patties with onions. Repeat for the second sandwich. Top with mayonnaise or burger sauce. Close the sandwiches and press gently to seat the layers. Serve immediately.
Serve immediately.
